title: Multi-step workflow optimization
use_when: Multi-step workflow optimization. Use when the user needs a product workflow for design & prototyping related to multi-step workflow optimization. Trigger terms: ux, workflow, friction, funnels, optimization.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Multi-step workflow optimization when the request is mainly metric diagnosis, pricing, GTM, or implementation planning without UX evidence; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Multi-step workflow optimization UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
framework_fit: Multi-step workflow optimization fits Design work in the Design lifecycle when the user needs Multi-step workflow optimization UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces Multi-step workflow optimization UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks without tying recommendations to the user's Design stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Multi-step workflow optimization as generic Design advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Multi-step workflow optimization when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $multi-step-workflow-optimization to turn this multi context into a decision-ready Multi-step workflow optimization UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks.
examples.expected_artifact: Multi-step workflow optimization UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
